# Open Health Dispositions

<!-- generated from docs/health/dispositions-events/; do not edit directly -->

| Event ID | Surface | Dimension | Object | Finding | Disposition | Date | Evidence | Closes |
|----------|---------|-----------|--------|---------|-------------|------|----------|--------|
| disp_20260623_000003 | tooling | quality | scripts/health_disposition_store.py | [matcher-skips-table-format] parse_findings_file only matches bold-bullet format; table-format finding rows are silently skipped (zero recall), so prior dispositions on them re-surface as new every sweep | accepted | 2026-06-23 | user accepted; proposed fix: extend parse_findings_file to handle table-format rows (or enforce one format) and print parsed N / file contains M with a mismatch warning |  |
| disp_20260623_000004 | tooling | quality | health audit lenses | [lens-false-positive-rate] 2026-06-23 sweep: raw 9 → verified 1 → dropped 8 (~89% false-positive rate); the ~190K-token evidence gate is load-bearing but fragile and the verification cost recurs every sweep | accepted | 2026-06-23 | user accepted; proposed fix: fold recurring drop reasons (prose-as-code, wrong-premise) back into originating lens prompts; add per-lens precision metric (verified/raw) so regression is visible |  |
| disp_20260623_000005 | tooling | design | scripts/health_static_lenses.py | [static-corpus-wider-than-lens-corpus] skill_files() globs every SKILL.md (filtering only archived), wider than the workflow:-contracted LLM lens set, surfacing structure findings on skills no LLM lens examined | accepted | 2026-06-23 | user accepted; proposed fix: apply workflow:-block filter to skill_files(), or document the asymmetry in discover-plugin-health Phase 2.5 and tag such findings |  |
| disp_20260623_000006 | tooling | design | discover-plugin-health | [discover-snippet-existence-gate] discover writes findings without checking the quoted snippet exists in the cited file; a fabricated quote reaches the report-phase evidence gate one stage and one verify dispatch too late | accepted | 2026-06-23 | user accepted; proposed fix: grep the quoted snippet against the cited file at discover before writing the finding |  |
| disp_20260623_000007 | tooling | design | scripts/health_disposition_store.py | [add-sync-shard-subcommand] append_row requires 8 hand-supplied args and no convenience subcommand exists, so shard sync re-keys a multi-sentence finding/note by hand, inviting quoting errors and divergence from the event store | accepted | 2026-06-23 | user accepted; proposed fix: add a sync_shard --event-id <id> (or --since) subcommand that reads the stored event and writes the matching shard row |  |
| disp_20260623_000008 | tooling | quality | discover-plugin-health | [per-dimension-lens-roster] remaining_lenses is computed for filtered runs but never enumerated, so a dimension-scoped run has no ground-truth count for the missing-lens completeness check | accepted | 2026-06-23 | user accepted; proposed fix: add a per-dimension lens roster table (dimension → LLM lenses + static lenses + expected total) to health-filter-contract.md |  |
| disp_20260623_000009 | tooling | quality | quality-agent-lens-clarity | [clarity-lens-nonconforming-reply] no anti-oscillation guard: the lens emitted a populated findings table then retracted to _No issues found._, giving contradictory verdicts in one reply | accepted | 2026-06-23 | user accepted; proposed fix: treat a clarity reply containing both a populated table and a trailing _No issues found._ as non-conforming and re-dispatch once; consider raising off haiku if oscillation reads as model-capacity |  |
| disp_20260623_000010 | tooling | quality | quality-agent-lens-clarity | [clarity-lens-prose-as-code] incomplete-conditional check flags every 'if X' with no else/otherwise in prose with no carve-out for unambiguous fall-throughs; a systematic category error that produced gate-dropped findings | accepted | 2026-06-23 | user accepted; proposed fix: add explicit exclusion — do not flag a missing else/otherwise branch in instructional prose when fall-through behaviour is unambiguous |  |
| disp_20260623_000011 | tooling | quality | scripts/health_static_lenses.py | [lens-completed-at-utc-vs-local] static runner stamps completed_at in UTC (+00:00) while every other date in the loop is local, manufacturing a false staleness signal inside a contract already heavy with staleness machinery | accepted | 2026-06-23 | user accepted; proposed fix: stamp completed_at in local time, or normalize zone at compare time; at minimum note in storage contract that completed_at is UTC |  |
| disp_20260623_000012 | tooling | quality | implement-plugin-health | [pretask-gitlog-needs-dash-one] Phase 1 pre-task commit-hash gate example uses 'git log --oneline <hash>' without -1, so it prints the entire ancestry when the gate only needs to confirm one hash resolves | accepted | 2026-06-23 | user accepted; proposed fix: change gate example to 'git log --oneline -1 <hash>'; verified against live file 2026-06-23 — still present |  |
| disp_20260623_000013 | tooling | design | health-discover-aggregation | [phase2-manifest-not-dimension-aware] manifest step prescribes writing all 11 derived context mappings unconditionally; on a quality-only run building the full manifest is wasted work | accepted | 2026-06-23 | user accepted; proposed fix: make Phase 2 manifest scope dimension-aware (file-list-only manifest when no derived context is needed) and document explicitly |  |
